_comment: Kurzfassungen der Regel-/Zauber-Texte fuer die Rueckseite und den Plan. 'summaries' = von Hand gepflegte Kurztexte (Schluessel = exakter Name). 'learned' = vom Tool automatisch gekuerzt (erster Satz) und gemerkt; bei Bedarf korrigieren oder nach 'summaries' verschieben. Ein Eintrag wird ueberall identisch verwendet (Karte + Plan).

learned.Blessings of the Lady: Once deployment is complete, instead of rolling off to determine which player takes the first turn, the Kingdom of Bretonnia army may kneel and pray for the Blessings of the Lady.
learned.Rallying Cry: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not engaged in combat, this character may nominate a single fleeing friendly unit that is within their Command range.
learned.The Grail Vow: A model with this Chivalrous Vow has the Immune to Psychology, Magical Attacks and Stubborn special rules.
learned.Virtue of Heroism: Unless using a magic weapon, a model with this Virtue gains the Killing Blow and Monster Slayer special rules.
learned.General: Inspiring Presence: Warriors fight all the better under the stern gaze of their General.
learned.Close Order: A unit consisting of models with this special rule may adopt a Close Order formation.
learned.Swiftstride: A unit which consists entirely of models with this special rule increases its maximum possible charge range by 3" and, before making a Charge, Flee or Pursuit roll, may choose to…
learned.Large Target: Large Targets do not benefit from partial or full cover.
learned.Fly (9): Except when following up or pursuing, a model with this special rule can choose to move by flying through the air, rather than moving across the ground as normal.
learned.Counter Charge: This special rule can only be used by units that consist entirely of models with this special rule.
learned.Stomp Attacks (D3): The number of Stomp Attacks caused varies from model to model and will be shown in brackets after the name of this special rule.
learned.Terror: Models with this special rule cause Terror.
learned.Grail Pendant: Models with the Grail Vow only.
learned.Lore of the Lady: A Wizard with the 'Lore of the Lady' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal.
learned.Aura of the Lady: Any unit this character has joined gains the Magical Attacks special rule.
learned.Magical Attacks: Any attack made or hit caused by a model with this special rule, or made using a weapon with this special rule, is a ‘Magical’ attack.
learned.Shield of the Lady: If this character has joined a unit that has a Unit Strength of 10 or more, and that has a Chivalrous Vow (see page 108), they may voluntarily ‘retire’ to the rear of the unit at…
learned.Magic Resistance (-2): The Casting roll of any enemy spell (including Bound Spells) that targets a unit that includes one or more models with this special rule suffers a modifier, as shown in brackets a…
learned.First Charge: If this unit’s first charge of the game is successful (i.e., if the unit makes contact with the charge target), the charge target becomes Disrupted until the end of the Combat pha…
learned.Finest Warhorses: When a unit with this special rule makes a Charge, Flee or Pursuit roll, it may re-roll any dice that roll a natural 1, before discarding any dice that are required to be discarde…
learned.Burning Gaze: Draw a straight line , 5D6" in length, from the caster's base edge.
learned.Fireball: The target enemy unit suffers 2D6 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -.
learned.Curse of Arrow Attraction: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, you may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 when shooting at the target enemy unit.
learned.Arcane Urgency: If the target friendly unit is not fleeing and has moved during this Movement phase, it may immediately move again.
learned.Oaken Shield: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the caster and any unit they have joined gain 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.
learned.Curse of Cowardly Flight: The target enemy unit must immediately make a Panic test.
learned.Hammerhand: A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers 2D3 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -2.
learned.The Knight's Vow: A model with this Vow does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly unit with the Peasantry special rule is destroyed whilst within 6" of it, or when it is fled through by a…
learned.Battle Standard Bearer: Armies often include a Battle Standard Bearer – a trusted warrior who holds aloft the General’s personal heraldry.
learned.War Banner: When calculating its combat result, a unit carrying the War Banner may claim an additional bonus of +1 combat result point.
learned.Impetuous: If during the Declare Charges & Charge Reactions sub-phase of its turn, a unit that includes one or more Impetuous models is able to declare a charge, it must make a Leadership te…
learned.Lance Formation: A unit consisting of models with this special rule may adopt a Lance formation.
learned.Monster Hunter's Tapestry: Enemy units may not make Stomp attacks against a unit carrying the Monster Hunter's Tapestry
learned.Levies: Models with this special rule cannot use the Inspiring Presence rule of the army’s General nor the Hold your Ground rule of a Battle Standard.
learned.Peasantry: If a unit with this special rule is within 6" of a friendly model that has the Knight’s Vow, the Questing Vow or the Grail Vow, and if that model is not fleeing, this unit can use…
learned.Skirmishers: A unit consisting of models with this special rule may adopt a Skirmish formation.
learned.Fly (10): Except when following up or pursuing, a model with this special rule can choose to move by flying through the air, rather than moving across the ground as normal.
learned.Dispersed Formation: Whilst in Skirmish formation, every model in a unit of Pegasus Knights must be within 2" of another model belonging to the same unit, rather than the usual 1".
learned.Furious Charge (Pegasus Knight & First K: During a turn in which it made a charge move of 3" or more, a model with this special rule gains a +1 modifier to its Attacks characteristic.
learned.Curse of the Necropolis: If a model with this special rule loses its last Wound to an enemy attack, the unit that made the attack must immediately make a Leadership test.
learned.Khopesh: A hand weapon carried by a model with this special rule has an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.
learned.Arise!: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not engaged in combat, this character may attempt to resurrect the fallen (see page 154) by making a Leadership test (using…
learned.Lore of Nehekhara: A Wizard with the 'Lore of Nehekhara' special rule may discard one of their randomly generated spells as normal.
learned.Indomitable (1): A unit with this special rule reduces the number of wounds suffered due to the Unstable special rule by the number shown in brackets (shown here as ‘X’).
learned.From Beneath the Sands: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, if they are not engaged in combat, this character may choose a single friendly unit that has both the Nehekharan Undead and the Ambushe…
learned.Nehekharan Undead: Models with this special rule are ‘Undead’.
learned.Regeneration (5+): Immediately after a Wound is lost, but before models with zero Wounds remaining are removed from play, a model with this special rule may make a "Regeneration save" roll by rollin…
learned.Vanguard: After deployment, units with this special rule may make a Vanguard move.
learned.Usekhp's Incantation of Desiccation: Until the end of this turn, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Strength and Toughness characteristics (to a minimum of 1).
learned.Khsar's Incantation of the Desert Wind: If this spell is cast with a casting result of 6 or more, a single friendly unit that has the Nehekharan Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Res…
learned.Djaf's Incantation of Cursed Blades: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, any friendly unit that has the Nehekhara Undead special rule and that is within the caster's Command Range might re-roll any rolls To Hit…
learned.Deathly Cabal: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the caster and any unit they have joined gain a 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack.
learned.Unquiet Spirits: The target enemy unit suffers 3D6 Strength 2 hits, with no armour save permitted (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).
learned.Curse of Years: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Movement, Weapon Skill and Toughness characteristic (to a minimum of 1).
learned.Spirit Leech: Until the end of this turn, the target enemy unit suffers a -2 modifier to its Leadership characteristic (to a minimum of 2) and cannot use their General's Inspiring Presence spec…
learned.Dwellers Bellow: Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is directly over the center of a unit the caster is engaged in combat with.
learned.Dry as Dust: Each time this model suffers an unsaved wound from a Flaming Attack, your opponent may roll a D6.
learned.Flammable: A model with this special rule cannot make a Regeneration save against a wound caused by a Flaming attack.
learned.Indomitable (2): A unit with this special rule reduces the number of wounds suffered due to the Unstable special rule by the number shown in brackets (shown here as ‘X’).
learned.My Will Be Done: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, this character may attempt to exert their will upon those around them by making a Leadership test (using their own Leadership).
learned.Reserve Move: Unless it charged, marched or fled during the Movement phase, a unit in which the majority of the models have this special rule may make a Reserve move at the end of the Shooting…
learned.Impact Hits (D3): The number of Impact Hits caused varies from model to model, and will be shown in brackets after the name of this special rile.
learned.Open Order: A unit consisting of models with this special rule may adopt an Open Order formation.
learned.Regeneration (6+): Immediately after a Wound is lost, but before models with zero Wounds remaining are removed from play, a model with this special rule may make a "Regeneration save" roll by rollin…
learned.Ambushers: A unit with this special rule may be held in reserve rather than be deployed at the start of the game.
learned.Arrows of Asaph: Units with this special rule never apply any modifiers to their rolls To Hit when shooting, regardless of the source of the modifier.
learned.Horde: A unit with this special rule may increase the maximum Rank Bonus it can claim (as determined by its troop type) by one.
learned.Regimental Unit: A unit with this special rule can be accompanied by ‘detachments’ (see page 282).
learned.Howdah: To represent its howdah and crew, a behemoth with this special rule has a split profile and follows both the ‘Split Profile (Chariots)’ and ‘Firing Platform’ rules (see page 194).
learned.Cleaving Blow (Tomb Guard Crew Only): If a model with this special rule rolls a natural 6 when making a roll To Wound for an attack made in combat, it has struck a ’Cleaving Blow’.
learned.Khopesh (Tomb Guard Crew Only): A hand weapon carried by a model with this special rule has an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.
learned.Timmm-Berrr!: When this model is reduced to zero Wounds, the winner of a roll-off chooses one of its arcs (front, flank or rear) for it to fall into.
learned.Unstoppable Assault: During the Combat phase of any turn in which this model charged, every attack it makes that causes an unsaved wound allows it to immediately make one additional attack.
learned.\"Hold The Line!\": This character and any unit they have joined automatically passes any Panic tests they are required to make.
learned.Stomp Attacks (D3+1): The number of Stomp Attacks caused varies from model to model and will be shown in brackets after the name of this special rule.
learned.Detachment: A unit with this special rule can be fielded as a ‘detachment’ (see page 282).
learned.Move Through Cover: Models with this special rule do not suffer any modifiers to their Movement characteristic for moving through difficult or dangerous terrain.
learned.Veteran: If the majority of the models in a unit have this special rule, the unit may re-roll any failed Leadership test.
learned.Handgun Drill: Once per game, each detachment of State Missile troops in a City-state of Nuln army can fire with one additional rank.
learned.Fast Cavalry: If all of the models (including characters) within a unit arrayed in an Open Order formation have this special rule, the unit may perform its Quick Turn (see page 183) even if it…
learned.Fire & Flee: If the majority of the models in a unit armed armed with missile weapons have this special rule, the unit may declare that it will ‘Fire & Flee’ as a charge reaction: Fire & Flee:…
learned.Grinding Wheels: Stomp Attacks made by a Steam Tank have an Armour Piercing characteristic of -2.
learned.Immune To Psychology: If the majority of the models in a unit are Immune to Psychology, the unit automatically passes any Fear, Panic or Terror tests it is required to make.
learned.Impact Hits (D6+1): The number of Impact Hits caused varies from model to model, and will be shown in brackets after the name of this special rile.
learned.Temperamental: If a natural 1 is rolled on either of the dice when the Steam Tank makes a Steam Power roll orn a Charge roll, the pressure has reached dangerous levels and, if not quickly releas…
learned.Unbreakable: If a unit with this special rule loses a round of combat, it is not required to make a Break test.
learned.Drilled: Unless it is fleeing, a Drilled unit may perform a free redress the ranks manoeuvre immediately before moving.
learned.Levitating Shield: Model whose troop type is 'infantry' only.
learned.Griffon Standard: When determining your combat result, a unit carrying the Griffon Standard can claim a Rank Bonus of +2 for each extra rank behind the first, rather than the usual +1.
learned.Fear: Models with this special rule cause Fear: • If a unit wishes to declare a charge against an enemy unit that both causes Fear and has a higher Unit Strength, it must first make a L…
learned.Order of the Fiery Heart: Models belonging to the Order of the Fiery Heart - Can only be joined by a Grand Master or Chapter Master belonging to the Order of the Fiery Heart, a Lector of Sigmar or a Priest…
learned.Clouds of Shoot & Smoke: Unless it is mounted, this model is considered to be behind full cover whilst it is within 3" of a friendly unit whose troop type is ‘war machine’
learned.Master of Ballistics: Unless this model is fleeing or engaged in combat, once per turn a friendly Empire war machine that is within its Command range can either use this model’s Ballistic Skill charact…
learned.Crushing Weight: Stomp attacks made by a War Wagon have an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.
learned.Impact Hits (D6+1, War Wagon only): The number of Impact Hits caused varies from model to model, and will be shown in brackets after the name of this special rile.
learned.Stable Firing Platform: To reflect the stability of a War Wagon, any missile weapons carried by the crew can be used in the Shooting phase even if the War Wagon marched this turn.
learned.Assorted Weapons: In addition to their hand weapons, each member of a War Wagon's crew is armed differently with each carrying one of the following six unique weapons: - Blunderbuss - Ball & Chain…
learned.Prayers of Sigmar: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, this character may attempt to invoke Sigmar’s blessings by chanting a single Prayer from the list below and making a Leadership test (u…
learned.Magic Resistance (-1): The Casting roll of any enemy spell (including Bound Spells) that targets a unit that includes one or more models with this special rule suffers a modifier, as shown in brackets a…
learned.Hammer of Sigmar: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, this model, its mount and a single friendly friendly unit that is within its Command range when tis Prayer is chanted may re-roll any roll…
learned.Shield of Faith: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, this model and a single friendly unit that is within its Command range when this Prayer is chanted has a 5+ Ward save against any wounds s…
learned.Soulfire: A single enemy unit this model is engaged in combat with immediately suffers D6 Strength 3 hits, each with an AP of -2.
learned.Stubborn: The first time this unit is required to make a Break test it may choose not to and will automatically Fall Back in Good Order instead, even if the Unit Strength of the winning sid…
learned.Feel No Pain: A model with this special rule has: - A 6+ Ward save against any wounds suffered that were caused by a non-magical enemy attack.
learned.Furious Charge: During a turn in which it made a charge move of 3" or more, a model with this special rule gains a +1 modifier to its Attacks characteristic.
learned.Hatred (all enemies): A model with this special rule may re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made against a hatred enemy during the first round of combat.
learned.Prayer of the Damned: During the Command sub-phase of their turn, this character may attempt to chant the Prayer of the Damned by making a Leadership test (using their own Leadership).
learned.Zealot: A character with this special rule can only join a unit with the Fanatical Zeal special rule.
learned.Storm Call: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to its Movement and Initiative characteristics (to a minimum of 1).
learned.Flaming Sword: A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers D6+1 Strength 3 hits, each with and AP of -.
learned.Plague of Rust: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -2 modifier to its armour value.
learned.Earthen Ramparts: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target friendly unit gains a 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered and counts as being behind a defended low linear obstacle if cha…
learned.Wind Blast: The target enemy unit suffers D3+3 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -1.
learned.Travel Mystical Pathway: If the target friendly unit is not fleeing and has not already moved during this Movement phase, you may immediately remove it from the battlefield and replace it anywhere complet…
learned.Order of the Knights Panther: Models belonging to the Order of the Knights Panther: - Must be armed with lances (note that models able to purchase magic weapons may do so as normal).
learned.Fanatical Zeal: A unit with this special rule can be joined by a Lector of Sigmar or a Priest of Sigmar.
learned.Hatred (All Enemies): A model with this special rule may re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made against a hatred enemy during the first round of combat.
learned.Dwarf Crafted: Models with this special rule do not suffer the usual -1 To Hit modifier when making a Stand & Shoot charge reaction.
learned.Hatred (Orcs & Goblins): A model with this special rule may re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made against a hatred enemy during the first round of combat.
learned.Resolute: Models with this special rule suffer a -1 modifier to the result of any Flee roll or Pursuit roll they make (to a minimum of 1).
learned.Ancestral Grudge: A model with this special rule has the Hatred (enemy characters) special rule, meaning it hates all characters in the opposing army.
learned.Gromril Armour: A model with this special rule may re-roll any roll of a natural 1 made when making an Armour Save roll
learned.Gromril Weapons: A hand weapon carried by a model with this special rule has an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.
learned.Borne Aloft: A model with Shieldbearers consists of not one, but four models – the character and three loyal retainers – occupying a single base and acting together as a single entity.
learned.Shieldwall: Once per game, during a turn in which it was charged, a unit with this special rule that is arrayed in a Close Order formation, and that is equipped with and chooses to use shield…
learned.Scouts: Units with this special rule may be deployed after all other units from both armies.
learned.Dive Bomb: Once per game, a unit with this special rule may perform a ‘Dive Bomb’ attack against a single enemy unit that is not engaged in combat.
learned.Royal Guard: Your army may include one unit of Hammerers for every King or Thane it includes.
learned.Stoic Defenders: During a turn in which it was charged by the enemy, a model with this special rule gains a +1 modifier to its Initiative and Attacks characteristics.
learned.Rune of Fear: A unit carrying a standard inscribed with the Rune of Fear gains the [Fear] special rule.
learned.Loner: A character with this special rule cannot be your General and cannot join a unit without this special rule.
learned.Scarab Prince: Should Prince Apophas lose his last Wound, before his model is removed from play, all enemy units within 2D6" of him suffer 2D6 Strength 2 hits with an AP of -1.
learned.Usirian’s Reaper: After deployment, nominate a single character in your opponent’s Muster List.
learned.Lore Familiar: The owner of a Lore Familiar does not randomly generate their spells.
learned.Fear of Elves: Elves of any type cause Fear in models with this special rule.
learned.Hatred (Dwarfs): A model with this special rule may re-roll any failed rolls To Hit made against a hatred enemy during the first round of combat.
learned.Warband: Unless it is fleeing, a Warband gains a positive (+) modifier to its Leadership characteristic equal to its current Rank Bonus, up to a maximum of Leadership 10.However, a Warband…
learned.Choppas: During a turn in which it charged, a model with this special rule may re-roll any rolls To Wound of a natural 1, and improves the Armour Piercing characteristic of its weapon(s) b…
learned.Ignore Goblin Panic: This unit does not have to make a Panic test when a friendly Goblin unit is destroyed or Breaks and flees from combat whilst within 6" of it.
learned.Waaagh!: Once per game, during the Command sub-phase of their turn, this character may attempt to invoke the power of the Waaagh!
learned.The Big Red Raggedy Flag: A unit carrying the Big Red Raggedy Flag has a +1 modifier to its Weapon Skill characteristic (to a maximum of 10).
learned.Furious Charge (does not apply to this m: During a turn in which it made a charge move of 3" or more, a model with this special rule gains a +1 modifier to its Attacks characteristic.
learned.Tusker Charge: During a turn in which it charged, a War Boar’s Tusks (hand weapon) have a Strength characteristic of S+1 and an Armour Piercing characteristic of -1.
learned.Armoured Hide: The hide of some creatures forms natural armour and improves their armour value ( and that of their rider).
learned.Chariot Runners: Friendly models whose troop type is ‘chariot’ can draw a line of sight over or through models with this special rule and can move through friendly units if they are in Skirmish fo…
learned.Feigned Flight: If this unit chooses to Flee (or Fire & Flee) as a charge reaction, it automatically rallies at the end of its move.
learned.Netters: When this unit’s combat is chosen during Step 1.1 of any Choose & Fight Combat sub-phase, it may attempt to ‘entangle’ one enemy unit it is engaged with.
learned.Release the Fanatics!: Fanatics are not placed on the battlefield at the start of the game.
learned.Fanatic Ball & Chain: Any unit (friend or foe) that is moved through by a moving Fanatic, or that moves through a Fanatic, suffers D6 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -3.
learned.Big 'Uns: When engaged in combat, a model with this special rule has a +1 modifier to its Strength characteristic and gains the Armour Bane(1) special rule.
learned.Da Angry Ladz Flag: A unit carrying Da Angry Ladz Flag gains the Frenzy special rule.
learned.Armoured Hide (1): The hide of some creatures forms natural armour and improves their armour value ( and that of their rider).
learned.Furious Charge (Orc Boar Boys & Boss Onl: During a turn in which it made a charge move of 3" or more, a model with this special rule gains a +1 modifier to its Attacks characteristic.
learned.Da Spider Banner: A unit carrying Da Spider Banner gains the Poisoned Attacks special rule.
learned.Armour Bane (1): If a model with this special rule rolls a natural 6 when making a roll To Wound, the Armour Piercing characteristic of its weapon is improved by the amount shown in brackets after…
learned.Stupidity: Unless it is fleeing or engaged in combat, a unit with this special rule must test for Stupidity by making a Leadership test during the Start of Turn sub-phase of each of its turn…
learned.Motley Crew: Units with this special rule may include models of the same type that are equipped differently to one another, and/or models of different types that fight together in a single uni…
learned.Doom Diver: When shooting a Doom Diver catapult, follow the Bombardment special rule as usual.
